data.title: Kyndryl slips as Q4 results, guidance come in-line with estimates
data.description:
Kyndryl (NYSE:KD) shares fell 1% in extended trading on Wednesday after the IT company reported fiscal fourth-quarter results that were largely in-line with estimates and initiated an outlook for fiscal 2026.

For the period ending March 31, the Martin Schroeter-led company reported earnings of $0.52 per share as revenue fell 1% year-over-year to $3.8B. In constant currency, revenue grew 1.3%, the company said. Kyndryl also generated $942M in cash flow from operations.

Kyndryl also bought back 1.8M shares during the period at a cost of $64M under its previously authorized program.

Analysts had expected earnings of $0.51 per share on $3.77B in revenue.

“Fiscal 2025 was another year of strong execution on our strategy,” Schroeter said in a statement. “In addition to returning to constant-currency revenue growth in the fourth quarter, we strengthened our leadership in innovative mission-critical technology services. We expanded our capabilities in cloud, modernization, applications, AI and security, and we further differentiated our services with Kyndryl Bridge.” 

Looking to fiscal 2026, Kyndryl, which was spun off by IBM (IBM) in 2021, expects revenue growth in constant currency of around 1%, at least $725M of adjusted pretax income and approximately $550M of adjusted free cash flow.

The company will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. EST tomorrow to discuss the results.

data.title: Skyworks Solutions appoints Mark Dentinger as CFO
data.description:
• Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) said on Wednesday that Mark Dentinger has been appointed senior vice president and chief financial officer, effective June 2, 2025. 
  • Dentinger succeeds Kris Sennesael, who is stepping down from his role as chief financial officer on May 9, 2025, to pursue another opportunity. 
  • In addition, Skyworks announced that Todd Lepinski has been appointed senior vice president, sales and marketing, effective June 2, 2025. 
  • Lepinski succeeds Carlos Bori, who will remain with the company in an advisory role for several months to ensure a smooth transition.
  • Dentinger recently served as CFO of Veritas, where he led the company’s global finance organization. 
  • Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) shares were up more than 2% in extended trading.
